+/// Owns a temporary Memory table used by a materialized CTE, and tracks whether
+/// the table has already been populated. The `is_built` flag is checked
+/// atomically in `MaterializingCTETransform` so that when the same CTE is
+/// referenced from multiple places (e.g. two IN-subqueries or an IN-subquery
+/// and the main plan), the table is written exactly once.
-    /// True iff the writer (`MaterializingCTETransform`) has finished

-    /// Non-blocking; pairs with the `memory_order_release` store performed
-    /// (planner, distributed executor) to decide whether the CTE's plan
-    /// must still be built or whether the storage is safe to read.
-    /// True iff `plan` is still held here OR the writer has finished. Used

-    /// once the plan has been claimed AND materialized, replanning would
-    /// create a detached duplicate that the runtime does not wire into the
/// If true, query plan is built for the CTE (i.e. the table is being populated, but is not ready for reads yet).

-    /// parallel to `is_materialization_planned`, but for the optimize
-    /// stage rather than the claim/move stage.

-    /// guarantees the writer has finished, so the load must observe `true`.
-    /// If it ever observes `false`, the planner failed to wire the gate -

/// Returns the subset of pre-built CTE plans that still need to be executed,
/// atomically marking each as materialized. CTEs already marked are skipped.
-    /// The plans must have already been optimized via `optimizePlans` in the
-    /// Optimize each owned CTE's pre-built plan. Called by
-    /// traversal then calls `makePlansForCTEs` to claim and attach.
-    /// Safe to call even after `makePlansForCTEs` has moved a CTE's plan
-    /// out â€” the per-CTE check `if (cte->plan)` makes the call a no-op
-    /// for CTEs whose plan has already been claimed (which happens when a
-    /// recursive `buildSetInplace` claims the same CTE first).
-/// Strip every `DelayedMaterializingCTEsStep` node from `plan`'s tree, at
-/// attaching a pre-built IN-subquery plan for runtime set construction; the
-/// strip forces the outer query plan to win `is_materialization_planned`
-/// for every referenced CTE so the outer `MaterializingCTEsStep`'s
-/// `DelayedPortsProcessor` becomes the single point that gates every
-/// `subqueries`, not in the immediate node tree) are not touched.
-/// Walk the plan tree and ask each `DelayedMaterializingCTEsStep` to optimize
-/// Returns `true` iff at least one `DelayedMaterializingCTEsStep` was found in
-/// the tree. This is the only place where `DelayedMaterializingCTEsStep::optimizePlans`
-/// is invoked, which in turn calls `materialized_cte->plan->optimize(...)` for
-/// each CTE the step owns. The recursive optimize call drives â€” via
-/// `addStepsToBuildSets` on the CTE's plan and via
-/// on any MergeTree read inside the CTE â€” the synchronous
-/// `FutureSetFromSubquery::buildSetInplace` path. When that path fires, the
-/// safety-net `DelayedMaterializingCTEsStep` planted inside the IN-subquery plan
-/// by `Planner.cpp`'s `forceMaterializeCTE` is the first to call
-/// `makePlansForCTEs` for the CTE, so it wins the
void resolveMaterializingCTEs(const QueryPlanOptimizationSettings & optimization_settings, QueryPlan & root_plan, QueryPlan::Node & root, QueryPlan::Nodes & nodes)
-    /// First traversal: optimize the pre-built plan of every materialized CTE
-    /// referenced by a `DelayedMaterializingCTEsStep` in the tree. The
-    /// optimize call may chain into `buildSetInplace`, which in turn invokes
-    /// `resolveMaterializingCTEs` on an IN-subquery's plan; that recursive
-    /// call gets to claim the CTE before we attempt the outer claim below.
-    /// If no DelayedMaterializingCTEsStep exists in the tree at all (the
-    /// common case for queries that do not use materialized CTEs) the second
-    /// traversal would be a guaranteed no-op, so we skip it. Traversal 1 does
-    /// not mutate the main plan tree â€” only the CTEs' own plans â€” so the set
-    /// of DelayedMaterializingCTEsStep nodes that traversal 2 would visit is
-    /// exactly the set traversal 1 already saw.
-    /// with a `MaterializingCTEsStep`, attaching the plans of CTEs that have
-    /// recursive `plan->optimize`) can materialize a referenced CTE through
-    /// in this plan is claimed. `resolveMaterializingCTEs` then only
-    /// materializes the CTEs that were not already materialized inplace.
static QueryPlan extractSubplan(Node * root, Nodes & nodes);
void replaceNodeWithPlan(Node * node, QueryPlan plan, SharedHeader expected_header);
-                /// `addPipelineBefore`) has already gated this reader on the
-                /// observe `is_built == false` here, the planner failed to
-                /// wire the gate - fail loudly rather than read from a
-                        "Reading from materialized CTE '{}' before its materialization completed - "
-                        "DelayedPortsProcessor gate is missing in the query plan",
+                    "Reading from materialized CTE '{}' before it has been materialized (materialization was planned: {})",
-static InputPorts createInputPorts(SharedHeader header, size_t num_streams, bool has_totals, bool has_extremes)
-DroppingTransform::DroppingTransform(SharedHeader header, size_t num_streams_, bool has_totals, bool has_extremes)
-    : IProcessor(createInputPorts(header, num_streams_, has_totals, has_extremes), OutputPorts(num_streams_, header))
-        /// Only count a pair as still-active after we know it is neither output-finished
-        /// nor (input-finished -> just finished here). Otherwise, when all inputs finish in
-        /// this same prepare() call, we would return PortFull and never be rescheduled to
-        /// report Finished, stalling the pipeline ("Pipeline stuck").
-            continue; /// PortFull for this stream; will be revisited.
-        /// Close any still-open aux inputs so upstream can stop.
-/** Consumes all output ports of a source pipeline: the data streams plus the optional
-  * totals and extremes streams. Forwards the data streams unchanged (1:1 pass-through)
-  * and drops (discards) the totals and extremes streams.
-  * Unlike a childless `NullSink` attached to the totals/extremes ports, this processor has
-  * data outputs that continue down the pipeline, so it is not a childless node. As a result
-  * `ExecutingGraph::initializeExecution` does not seed it, which avoids prematurely pulling
-  * Port order in the constructor: first the `num_streams` data inputs, then (if present) the
-  * totals input, then (if present) the extremes input. Outputs are the `num_streams` data
-  * outputs. This ordering is required by `Pipe::addTransform`, which connects the pipe's data
-  * ports to the first inputs and identifies the totals/extremes inputs by pointer.
-    DroppingTransform(SharedHeader header, size_t num_streams_, bool has_totals, bool has_extremes);
